MOVING AND OPPOSING PARTIES APPEARED. A R G U E D AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH ONE MODIFICATION -- THAT REAL PARTY IN INTEREST SUPERIOR ACCESS INSURANCE SERVICES IS TO TURN OVER $1,646.022.63 TO THE INSURANCE COMMISSIONER. MR. GREENBERG MAY FILE A DECLARATION SETTING FORTH WHY HIS CLIENT CANNOT COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDER. BOTH PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER AFTER THE FILING OF THE DECLARATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. HONORABLE A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II. REPORTER: PATRICIA M. EVEZICH, CSR 1193 (GPS)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Caroline Lam, Esq. (213.269.6224; caroline.lam@doj.ca.gov) Deputy Attorney General for Moving Party.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER INSURANCE COMMISSIONER OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Reopen Proceedings For Limited Purpose And Motion For Order Approving Distribution Of Surplus Funds To Approved Class 2 Claimants:

Plaintiff's unopposed "motion to reopen proceedings for limited purpose and motion for order approving distribution of surplus funds to approved class 2 claimants" are granted.

Order is signed.

MOTION Verified Application For Order To Show Cause Why Estate Assets Should Not Be Turned Over To Insurance Commissioner Pursuant To Order Of Liquidation And Why Superor Access Insurance Services, Inc. Shou
Off Calendar Mar-05-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
A P P E A R A N C E S:
David Lew, Deputy Attorney General 510/622-2225 for applicant INSURANCE COMMISSIONER OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA moving for an order to show to show cause as to why estate assets should not be turned over to the Insurance Commissioner purusant to order of liquidation and why SUPERIOR ACCESS INSURANCE SERVICES, INC. shuld not be held in contempt of Court
Raymond A. Green berg, Esq. 818/876-8540 for Real Party In Interest SUPERIOR ACCESS INSURANCE SERVICES, INC. opposing party
A R G U E D and the Court adopted its tentative ruling with one modification -- that Real Party In Interest SUPERIOR ACCESS INSURANCE SERVICES is to turn over $1,646.022.63 to the Insurance Commissioner. Mr. Greenberg may file a declaration setting forth why his client cannot comply with the Court's order. Both parties are ordered to meet and confer after the filing of the declaration. Order signed in open court.
(AJR/md) Patricia M. Evezich, CSR 1193
